subject: Sociology
title: The Underclass Debate
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Underclass Debate.

hook.text: Are the very poor trapped by culture or by structure? This question has split sociologists for decades.

buildup.text: The underclass refers to the persistently poor: long-term unemployed, often concentrated in segregated neighborhoods.

discovery.text: Oscar Lewis proposed a culture of poverty: values that trap families across generations in self-defeating patterns.

twist.text: William Julius Wilson countered: deindustrialization destroyed jobs. No culture shift causes mass joblessness.

climax.text: The debate matters because the answer shapes policy: job programs vs. behavior interventions target different causes.

punchline.text: Blaming culture is easier. Fixing structures is harder — and more effective.

quiz.question: What did William Julius Wilson argue about persistent poverty?
options: Deindustrialization destroyed jobs and created the underclass
A culture of poverty traps families
Poverty is entirely a personal choice
